name: Cadence Diagnostic Intake
description: Routes users into one of four protocol variants (A/B/C/D) based on validated and self-designed assessments.

sections.title: Premature Ejaculation Diagnostic Tool (PEDT-5)
sections.description: Five quick questions used by clinicians worldwide to assess ejaculation control. Answer honestly — there are no right answers, and your responses stay on this device.
sections.internal_note: Validated 5-item screening tool from Symonds et al. (2007). Each item scored 0-4. Total ≥11 indicates PE; 9-10 probable PE; ≤8 unlikely.

questions.text: How difficult is it for you to delay ejaculation?
Do you ejaculate before you want to?
Do you ejaculate with very little stimulation?
Do you feel frustrated because of ejaculating before you want to?
How concerned are you that your time to ejaculation leaves your partner sexually unfulfilled?

sections.title: Pelvic Floor Tension Self-Assessment
sections.description: These questions help us check whether your pelvic floor is chronically tense — a common pattern that's often missed and that responds to a different kind of training. There's no right answer; just be honest.
sections.internal_note: Identifies signs of a chronically tense (hypertonic) pelvic floor. Standard Kegel exercises can worsen symptoms in this profile, so this section is the safety-critical routing input. ≥3 yes responses indicates hypertonic likely.

questions.text: Do you frequently notice yourself clenching your pelvic, glute, or abdominal muscles when stressed or anxious?
Do you experience tension, tightness, or discomfort in your perineum, groin, or lower pelvis (not during sex)?
Do you have noticeably tight hip flexors or limited hip mobility (e.g. difficulty squatting deep, sitting cross-legged comfortably)?
Do you experience urinary urgency, frequency, or a feeling of incomplete emptying?
Do you experience discomfort, numbness, or tension after sitting for long periods (e.g. >30 minutes)?
Do you experience pain, tension, or a 'painful contraction' feeling during or shortly after ejaculation?
When you try to push your pelvic floor outward (as if starting to urinate), is it difficult or unfamiliar to do?

sections.title: Masturbation & Stimulus Patterns
sections.description: Masturbation habits — pace, grip, and stimulus type — shape how your body responds during sex. These questions help us understand your patterns and tailor your protocol. Skip any that don't apply.
sections.internal_note: Identifies porn-conditioned and high-intensity masturbation patterns associated with sensitization (Variant D).

questions.text: How often do you currently use pornography?
When you masturbate, how would you describe your grip pressure?
When you masturbate, how would you describe your pace?
When you masturbate without pornography, how difficult do you find it to become aroused or climax?

options.label: Not difficult — I rarely or never use porn anyway

questions.help_text: Answer based on your experience when you have tried, or imagine trying. If you've never masturbated without porn, choose 'Almost always difficult'.

questions.text: Did you previously use pornography heavily for an extended period (a year or more), even if you don't currently?
questions.help_text: We ask because patterns established during heavy use can persist long after someone stops. This helps tailor your protocol.
questions.internal_note: Identifies users with residual porn-conditioned grip/pace patterns who would otherwise mis-route to Variant C or A.

options.label: No — I never used porn heavily
Yes — but I stopped or significantly reduced over a year ago
Yes — I stopped or significantly reduced within the last year
Yes — and I still use it currently

sections.title: Onset
sections.description: When the issue started matters. Long-standing patterns and recent ones often respond to different approaches.
sections.internal_note: Critical for distinguishing lifelong (more neurobiological) from acquired (more situational/anxiety-driven) PE.

questions.text: When did your concerns about ejaculation timing begin?

options.label: After a period of normal control (acquired)
I'm not sure / it has varied

sections.title: Baseline
sections.description: A starting point we'll measure against. Don't overthink — your best estimate is fine.
sections.internal_note: Captures starting IELT estimate as the primary outcome metric.

questions.text: On average, how long do you typically last during partnered intercourse, from penetration to ejaculation?

options.label: Not currently sexually active with a partner

questions.text: Would you describe yourself as significantly anxious or worried about your sexual performance?

sections.title: Personalize Your Protocol
sections.description: Your variant is set. These optional add-ons let you go deeper in areas you care about — without changing the core protocol.
sections.internal_note: Optional add-ons. Each maps to a flag the program engine reads when assembling the daily plan. The core variant protocol is always primary; add-ons appear as supplementary sessions. Note: questions are filtered by variant — only questions with the user's variant in applies_to_variants are shown.

questions.text: Would you like to include supplementary pelvic floor training in your routine?
questions.internal_note: Variant A and D protocols only include light PFM work. Some users still want it. Adds 2-3 short PFM sessions per week as supplementary, never replacing core variant content.

options.label: Yes — add a few short pelvic floor sessions per week
No — stick to the recommended protocol

questions.text: Would you like extra breathwork and parasympathetic training sessions?
questions.internal_note: Variant B already has heavy breath/relaxation focus, so this is mostly relevant for C and D users who want more. For A users, breathwork is already the core, so we don't ask.

options.label: No — stick to the recommended protocol

questions.text: Would you like to add daily mindfulness sessions (5-10 min) alongside your protocol?
questions.internal_note: Universal add-on. Mindfulness has independent evidence for sexual function (Brotto et al.) and stacks well with all four variants.

options.label: No — stick to the recommended protocol

questions.text: How much time can you realistically commit per day?
questions.internal_note: Determines session pacing. 5-min users get the minimum-viable session; 15-min users get the full session with all extras inline.
